name: Granola AI MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Granola AI MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Granola — the AI-powered meeting notes application — retrieving meeting notes and transcripts, searching past meetings, and integrating Granola's automatically structured meeting knowledge into agent-driven productivity and meeting follow-up workflows.
use_cases: Retrieving meeting notes from past discussions from research agents
Searching for decisions made in previous meetings from follow-up agents
Extracting action items and commitments from meeting records from task agents
Building context from recent meetings for current work from planning agents
Summarizing project meeting history from status update agents
Finding meeting participants and discussion context from relationship agents
not_for: Teams not using Granola for meeting notes
Real-time meeting transcription (Granola processes after meeting)
Teams using Otter.ai, Fireflies, or other meeting tools
best_when: A Granola user wants AI agents to access their meeting history — retrieving past decisions, action items, and discussions from structured meeting notes.
avoid_when: You don't use Granola or prefer other meeting note tools.

auth.notes: Granola API token required. Available from Granola app settings. Mac application must be installed and running.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Granola: free tier with limited meetings per month
pricing.notes: Official MCP from granola-inc. Granola app subscription required for active use. Free tier available.

known_agent_gotchas: Granola Mac app required — no Windows or web-only option for MCP
Meeting data must be actively captured by Granola during meetings
Meeting notes may contain sensitive business discussions
Granola requires microphone access — privacy consideration
macOS only for now — limits team adoption
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Granola API token. Official MCP. Meeting notes contain sensitive discussions — treat with care.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS. API token. Official MCP. Meeting notes are sensitive.
